title: First Blood
teaches: the fight phase: both sides fight, and the casualties you inflict first never swing back.
intro: Every battle so far was settled at rifle range. Not this one. Your Ork Boyz stand eye to eye with a squad of Space Marine Intercessors — this is the fight phase, and it plays by different rules: BOTH sides fight in it, every engaged unit must swing, and the players take turns picking who swings next. You pick first, because it is your turn. Watch two things: the hit rolls now use Weapon Skill (WS) instead of Ballistic Skill, and when the Marines answer, count the dice in their ATTACKS line — every Marine you cut down before then never gets to make his.

turns.narrate_before: 'Ere we go! The Boyz crash into the Marines, choppas swinging. Ten Boyz, three attacks each — thirty dice, hitting on the Boyz' Weapon Skill of 3+. Then whoever is still standing in blue gets to answer.

outro: That was the fight phase, and it showed you three things shooting never could. First: the hit roll used Weapon Skill, not Ballistic Skill — Boyz are terrible shots (BS 5+) but vicious up close (WS 3+), which is why Orks want this fight and Marines don't. Second: melee always answers back — both sides fought in the same phase, and fighting is not optional. Third, and most important: the answer came from the survivors only. The Marines brought five models and fifteen attacks to this fight — count the dice they actually rolled. Striking first is not a bonus die or a modifier; it is attrition, because a dead model makes no attacks. You struck first because it was your turn. In the full game, CHARGING is how you seize that right on someone else's — that arrives with movement in a later update. Replay with a few different seeds and watch how the whole exchange tilts on those opening swings.
